title: The Importance of Being Earnest: A Trivial Comedy for Serious People
author: Wilde, Oscar
summary: The Importance of Being Earnest is a comic play in which two friends, John Worthing (Jack) and Algernon, invent fictitious personas to escape social obligations. Jack pretends to have a mischievous brother “Ernest” in town while Algernon invents an invalid called Bunbury to visit the country. Mistaken identities, cigarette-case evidence, guardianship of young Cecily, and flirtations with Gwendolen produce witty banter, social satire, and critiques of marriage and morality. The characters spar over propriety, engagements, and family obligations amid London society, with servants, a stern aunt, a governess, and gossiping relatives complicating intentions and revealing hypocrisy. Wit and farce abound.
